photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: a new type of food additive is discovered to be causing cancer in humans
megalodon shark sightings off the coast
a military coup overthrows the government of a nuclear-armed nation in a sudden overnight raid
a massive new environmental law is passed
a massive, coordinated prison break releases hundreds of high-security inmates simultaneously
a revolutionary new form of renewable energy is discovered

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a prominent social media influencer is banned from major platforms, sparking intense debate on free speech
a mass protest against police brutality and systemic racism
a historic museum heist occurs, with thieves stealing priceless masterpieces overnight
a major cultural festival, celebrating the traditions and customs of a particular group or region
a historic moment, such as the first manned mission to mars or the discovery of a new planet
a major food recall is issued after a widespread foodborne illness outbreak is linked to a popular crop

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a devastating building collapse: a multi-story residential building in mumbai collapses overnight, triggering a frantic search for survivors
a major environmental crisis: a massive oil spill off the coast of brazil threatens wildlife and fishing communities, leading to calls for greater regulation of the oil industry
a massive wildfire: a fire sweeps through the australian outback, destroying homes and forcing thousands of residents to evacuate
a massive refugee migration: thousands of families flee gang violence in central america, forming a massive caravan traveling toward the border
a catastrophic flooding event: heavy monsoon rains in bangladesh submerge entire villages, displacing millions of residents
a major world crisis: the covid-19 pandemic sweeps across the globe, leading to millions of deaths and upending daily life for billions of people

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: intentional camera movement: deliberately moving the camera during a longer exposure to create a dynamic, abstract representation of chaos and action
photojournalistic essay: using a series of photographs to tell a story about the protests and the issues they are addressing
analog film photography: using traditional 35mm or medium format film to create a raw, grain-textured aesthetic with natural color tones
extreme wide-angle photography: using wide lenses to include both the protesters in the foreground and the grand architectural context in the background
documentary photography: providing a historical record of the protests and the people involved
retro photography: using vintage or outdated equipment to create a unique and stylized look

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: coverage of anti-government and pro-democracy protests
photojournalistic documentation of the impact of gentrification and urban development on communities
advocacy photojournalism, documenting specific causes to raise awareness and inspire social change
coverage of student protests and movements, including those for gun control and against student debt
coverage of political rallies and protests

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A fencer engaged in a fast-paced duel.
A giraffe in a lifeguard outfit, sitting atop a high chair and watching over a crowded pool.
An adorable happy otter confidently stands on a surfboard wearing a yellow lifejacket, riding along turquoise tropical waters near lush tropical islands, 3D digital render art style.
Historical footage of California during the gold rush.
A haunted mansion with flickering candles and eerie shadows.
A slow-motion video of a liquid droplet bouncing on a water-repellent surface.

set.parti.title: PartiPrompts
set.parti.description: PartiPrompts (P2) is a rich set of over 1600 prompts in English. P2 can be used to measure model capabilities across various categories and challenge aspects.

several people putting their hands on a basketball
the moon with a smiling face
a red sphere on top of a yellow box
a circular brown trash bin in front of a brick wall
